message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: El Mexicali Cafe (Indio)
Cuisine/type signal: Mexican
Why it surfaced in discovery: Opened 1981 on Indio Blvd; one of the longest-running woman-owned eateries in the Coachella Valley; now spans four generations operating El Mexicali Cafe and El Mexicali Cafe II; family-recipe menu, seafood specialties.
Discovery sources: https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/el-mexicali-cafe-indio/26631/ | http://elmexicalicafe2.com/ | https://www.yelp.com/biz/el-mexicali-cafe-indio-3

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

El Mexicali Cafe was first opened by Carmen Murguia in 1981. Her daughter, Monica, has taken over, continuing the family legacy. 

There are now two El Mexicali Cafe locations, with the second location run by Monica's sister Laura at 43430 Monroe Street.

El Mexicali Cafe is the oldest woman-owned restaurant in the Coachella Valley, and in 2016, it was honored as the 56th Assembly District Small Business of the Year.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Mexican restaurants in Indio and the Coachella Valley:

## Top Recommendations

**El Tranvia Restaurant**
El Tranvia remains the area's go-to spot for delicious Mexican food. The quesabirria tacos are exceptional, and highlights include tacos gobernador (shrimp and cheese taco), costillas con nopales (pork ribs with cactus), and chicken mole. Located at 45334 Golf Center Parkway, Indio.

**Soul of Mexico**
Soul of Mexico is known for its red enchilada sauce, vegetable stack appetizer, and governator tacos. Located at 44-100 Jefferson Street, Indio.

**Jalisco Restaurant**
Jalisco Restaurant, a family-owned Mexican eatery in Indio, has been a favorite in the Coachella Valley for years and is renowned for its authentic Mexican fare, offering tacos, burritos, fajitas.

**El Tapatio Restaurant**
El Tapatio is renowned for offering some of the best Mexican and seafood in the valley, with ceviche showcasing fresh seafood and fish tacos that are crisp and flavorful. Located at 83161 Indio Blvd.

**El Mexicali Cafe**
El Mexicali Cafe, located at 82720 Indio Blvd, is a beloved staple in the Coachella Valley since 1981, known for its warm, family-friendly atmosphere.

Other notable mentions include **Macario's Restaurant**, **El Campanario Restaurant**, **Tacos Gonzalez**, and **Rosamarillo's**, all offering authentic Mexican cuisine with various specialties ranging from traditional dishes to seafood preparations.

Based on the search results, here's information about El Mexicali Cafe in Indio from various review sources:

## Overview

El Mexicali Cafe, opened in 1981, is one of the longest-running woman-owned eateries in the Coachella Valley. The restaurant actually has multiple locations:

- **Original Location**: 82720 Indio Blvd, Indio, CA 92201
- **El Mexicali Cafe II**: 43430 Monroe St Ste A, Indio, CA 92201

## Reviews Summary

**From Yelp and TripAdvisor:**
- Established in 1981
- Health Score: 94 out of 100

**Customer Praise:**
Visitors note that the restaurant is family oriented, service is excellent, and the food amazing. Guests praise its "authentic" and "real Mexican food," calling it "the best" and "above and beyond excellent."

**Signature Dishes:**
Chiles Gueritos are a popular local favorite - shrimp stuffed yellow peppers topped with mayo and soy sauce. The chile guerritos are described as "absolute fire" and "hands down the best in the desert."

**Unique Atmosphere:**
The "Shake, Rattle, and Roll" of the trains passing by on the train tracks behind the restaurant is something you'll never forget.

**Amenities:**
Happy Hour is offered 3-5 pm every Monday-Friday with specials on wine, tequila shots, draft beer, select cocktails, and appetizers.

# El Mexicali Café: Key Information

**Founder & Ownership:**
Carmen Murguia established the restaurant in 1981. Her daughter Monica currently operates the original location, while Monica's sister Laura runs the second location.

**Years Operating:**
The restaurant celebrated its 40th anniversary in 2021, indicating continuous operation since 1981.

**Location Details:**
- Original location: 82720 Indio Boulevard, Indio
- Second location: 43430 Monroe Street

**Notable Features:**
The establishment is recognized as "the oldest woman-owned restaurant in the Coachella Valley," according to Mayor Elaine Holmes.

**Recognition & Awards:**
- City of Indio proclamation celebrating the 40th anniversary (March 2021)
- Certificate of recognition from State Senator Melissa Melendez

**Signature Offerings:**
The restaurant is known for "authentic home style cooking" and specialty items like chiles gueritos served with mayo and soy sauce, accompanied by railroad-themed ambiance.

**Resilience:**
The family demonstrated business continuity despite the COVID-19 shutdown, celebrated one year after the closure period.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about El Mexicali Cafe in Indio:

## Overview
El Mexicali Cafe in Indio has been discovered as a hidden gem, and it opened in 1981 and is one of the longest-running woman-owned eateries in the Coachella Valley.

## Signature Dishes
The restaurant is particularly known for its unique dishes:

- **Chiles Gueritos**: These are a popular local favorite - shrimp stuffed yellow peppers topped with mayo and soy sauce

- **Seafood**: They offer fresh seafood dishes like Huachinango Frito Entero, along with other seafood specialties

## Restaurant Details
The restaurant specializes in serving authentic Mexican food from secret family recipes, including seafood specialties, plus cocktails from a full bar. The restaurant offers a unique dining experience that's just like eating at home with family and friends.

content.content:
# El Mexicali Café Recognition

**Restaurant:** El Mexicali Café II

**Award:** USA TODAY's 2026 Restaurants of the Year

**Issuing Organization:** USA TODAY

**Date:** February 11, 2026

**Owner Quote:** "We were excited, of course, to be part of this. It boosts the business and our economy here and is showcasing us." — Laura Sandez

# Restaurant Recognition Summary

**Restaurant:** El Mexicali Cafe II (located in Indio)

**Date:** February 11, 2026

**Recognition:** Named to USA TODAY's Restaurants of the Year 2026 list, which features 39 establishments nationwide.

**Selection Criteria:** The restaurant was chosen for its "festive ambiance, memorable margaritas and famous appetizer consisting of peppers stuffed with shrimp."

**Owner's Response:** Laura Sandez, the owner, expressed enthusiasm about the honor, noting that such recognition "boosts our economy here and the business."

content.text: Note: The discovery framing was about El Mexicali Cafe (Indio Blvd, the original). The USA Today award went to El Mexicali Cafe II (Monroe St). They're family-related but technically different entities. I'll note this carefully.

input.name: El Mexicali Cafe (Indio)
input.cuisine_or_type: Mexican (family-recipe, seafood specialties, signature chiles gueritos)
input.corridor: festival-adjacent Indio (Indio Blvd corridor; original at 82720 Indio Blvd)

reputation.review_volume: Yelp ~415 reviews at original (82720 Indio Blvd); sibling El Mexicali Cafe II at ~601 reviews. Combined Yelp footprint 1000+ across the family.
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive — recurring praise for authenticity, family-style service, chiles gueritos (shrimp-stuffed yellow peppers w/ mayo + soy), seafood, and railroad-track ambiance
reputation.recency_signal: Recent coverage and reviews remain favorable through 2026; USA Today Restaurants of the Year 2026 (Feb 11, 2026) brought a fresh wave of visibility to the El Mexicali Cafe II sibling location
reputation.longevity: Opened 1981 by Carmen Murguia; 40th anniversary recognized by City of Indio (March 2021); now operated across multiple generations by daughters Monica (original) and Laura Sandez (II). 45+ years continuous operation.
credentials: Certificate of recognition from State Senator Melissa Melendez (2021)
56th Assembly District Small Business of the Year (2016) [UNVERIFIED — surfaced in search summary, not directly fetched]
USA TODAY Restaurants of the Year 2026 — awarded to El Mexicali Cafe II sibling location, not the original 82720 Indio Blvd entity
Listed by Visit Greater Palm Springs (official DMO)

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best Mexican restaurants Indio' and 'longest-running Mexican Coachella Valley' and 'oldest woman-owned restaurant Coachella Valley.' Surfaces in Palm Springs Life reader picks and Visit Greater Palm Springs DMO listings. Does NOT consistently surface for broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'date night' regional queries — gets crowded out by El Paseo and Palm Springs corridor names. The USA Today 2026 halo lands almost entirely on the El Mexicali Cafe II sibling, not the original — agents conflate the two.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Mostly accurate — most listings correctly attribute 1981 founding and woman-owned status. Common confusion: agents conflate the original (Indio Blvd) with El Mexicali Cafe II (Monroe St), attributing the USA Today award and Laura Sandez to the original. Founder occasionally misnamed (e.g., 'Lourdes Marquez' surfaced in discovery framing; verified founder is Carmen Murguia).
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Own site (elmexicalicafe.com and elmexicalicafe2.com) could not be fetched directly — elmexicalicafe.com returned certificate-expired (TLS cert lapsed), elmexicalicafe2.com pages also TLS-broken. This is itself a major agent-readability problem: HTTPS-strict crawlers and LLM fetchers will skip or fail on the canonical sources. No JSON-LD verification possible. Menu surfaces via third-party aggregators (Sirved, NetWaiter, findmenuworld, postcard.inc, SpotOn) rather than canonical site, which dilutes signal.

competitors: El Tranvia Restaurant (Indio) — quesabirria + tacos gobernador heavyweight
El Tapatio Restaurant (Indio Blvd, same corridor) — Mexican + seafood overlap
Soul of Mexico (Indio) — modern Mexican challenger
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — for the festival-adjacent Indio / Indio Blvd corridor it is the heritage anchor (1981, woman-owned, multi-generational). For the broader Coachella Valley dining map it functions as a supporting reference rather than a top-of-mind regional anchor.

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent — family-owned across four generations, founded by Carmen Murguia; daughters Monica and Laura Sandez operate the two locations. No chain or resort affiliation.
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Mixed/light — owns two distinct domains (elmexicalicafe.com, elmexicalicafe2.com) but both currently have expired TLS certificates, signaling deferred web maintenance. Paid/managed listings on Grubhub, SpotOn ordering, Sirved, NetWaiter suggest some third-party platform spend. Active local press engagement (KESQ, USA Today pickup). No visible agency footprint or paid SEM evidence.
convertibility.reasoning: Independent, multi-generational, woman-owned family business with a reachable decision-maker (Monica or Laura Sandez are publicly identified operators and have spoken to press as recently as Feb 2026). They have demonstrably engaged with marketing channels — two websites, third-party ordering platforms, press outreach — but the expired TLS certificates on both domains plus reliance on aggregator menus indicate the digital layer is under-resourced relative to their reputation. The USA Today 2026 award created a visibility surge that is not yet reflected on their own site. Classic convertible profile: real reputation, real marketing willingness, real gap to close.

flags: Discovery framing said 'now spans four generations' — verified press only confirms second/third-generation operators (Carmen Murguia → daughters Monica and Laura Sandez). Four-generation claim [UNVERIFIED].
Discovery framing implied founder 'Lourdes Marquez' — verified founder is Carmen Murguia (per KESQ). Lourdes Marquez attribution appears to be incorrect.
56th Assembly District Small Business of the Year 2016 [UNVERIFIED] — surfaced in a search-engine summary but not directly fetched from a primary source.

USA TODAY Restaurants of the Year 2026 award was given to El Mexicali Cafe II (43430 Monroe St) operated by Laura Sandez, NOT the original El Mexicali Cafe at 82720 Indio Blvd. Halo benefits the family brand but is not strictly the discovery entity.

Both canonical domains (elmexicalicafe.com, elmexicalicafe2.com) returned expired-TLS errors during fetch — could not directly verify on-site schema.org / JSON-LD presence, current hours, or menu structure. [UNVERIFIED on-site structured data].
